commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Soul.in birds The covenant of God is made with animals, only in as much as they are subservient to man. (Du Hamel)
The Egyptians adored most of them; and many oriental nations, and even philosophers, pretended they had intelligent souls, and could speak a rational language, which some of them would have the people believe they could understand. (Calmet)
This was the case of those great impostors Apollonius of Tyena, Mahomet (Haydock)
Moses shows sufficiently that beasts were neither divinities nor rational. (Calmet)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
